bullets.text: A roof collapse at a tuition centre in Lahore, Pakistan, has killed 14 children, leading to the arrest of two individuals.
The whereabouts of nearly 300 people who have tested positive for Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo are unknown, raising fears of widespread community transmission.
In South Africa, police units have been deployed across the country ahead of anti-immigration marches, with fears of a repeat of the 2008 anti-migrant violence.
Teenagers across Australia report that vapes remain easily accessible and commonplace despite a recent crackdown, according to an ABC investigation.
Scientists in Australia are preparing to release an imported plant pathogen, a natural enemy, to combat one of the country's most problematic weeds.
New research highlights Queensland's significant skin cancer burden, with middle-aged residents undergoing nearly 1.5 million procedures annually.
The World Bank is set to gradually stop lending to China, a decision that follows years of pressure from the Trump administration.

US stocks have achieved their largest quarterly gain in six years, with investors navigating various factors including the fallout from the Iran war, volatility in chip stocks, and a significant SpaceX IPO.
Nike's shares fell after executives offered cautious commentary on future demand, despite the company's latest quarterly results surpassing expectations.
